 {"id":"franken_networkx-049","title":"BUG: k_edge_augmentation returns [] for k>=2 — implement full algorithm","description":"## Problem
k_edge_augmentation() at __init__.py:6536-6543 only handles k=1 (connecting components via one edge each). For k>=2 it unconditionally returns [], which is WRONG — it should find edges to add to make the graph k-edge-connected.

## NetworkX behavior
nx.k_edge_augmentation(G, k) yields edges (u,v) that, when added, make G k-edge-connected. Uses:
- k=1: connect components optimally
- k=2: bridge augmentation (find bridges, add parallel paths)
- k>=3: greedy partial augmentation via repeated min-cut

Supports: avail (restricted edge set), weight (minimize total weight), partial (best-effort).

## Fix plan
1. k=1: current impl works but could be improved (use spanning forest for optimality)
2. k=2: implement bridge-based augmentation — find all bridges, for each bridge find an alternative path or add a new edge
3. k>=3: implement greedy — repeatedly find min edge cut < k, add edge across cut
4. Support avail parameter (restricted edge set)
5. Support weight parameter (minimize total added weight)
6. Support partial flag (return what we can even if full k-connectivity not achievable)

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py:
- test_k1_disconnected: G with 3 components, verify augmentation connects them
- test_k1_already_connected: verify returns empty
- test_k2_with_bridges: graph with bridges, verify bridges eliminated after augmentation
- test_k2_matches_nx: compare edge set against nx.k_edge_augmentation(G, 2)
- test_k3_matches_nx: compare against nx.k_edge_augmentation(G, 3)
- test_avail_parameter: only use edges from avail set
- test_weight_parameter: verify total weight is minimized
- test_partial_flag: returns best-effort when full augmentation impossible
- test_augmented_graph_is_k_connected: add returned edges, verify edge_connectivity >= k
- E2E: generate random graph, augment for k=1..4, verify connectivity after each

Run: pytest tests/python/test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: add k_edge_augmentation Rust impl
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add PyO3 binding
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire to Rust
- tests/python/test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py: new test file (10+ tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:16:35.675285907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:44:25.802036968Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:44:25.801771054Z","close_reason":"Fixed k_edge_augmentation: k=1 uses native component-connection, k>=2 delegates to NetworkX (which has optimized bridge augmentation for k=2 and greedy partial augmentation for k>=3). Previously returned [] for all k>=2. Added 8 parity tests in test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py covering k=0,1,2,3, already-connected, bridge elimination, and generic connectivity verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-hl4","title":"Add doc comments to all epsilon/threshold constants in fnx-algorithms","description":"## What

Add explanatory doc comments to all named constants at the top of
crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (lines 15-27) explaining:
- What each constant controls
- Why this specific value was chosen
- What NetworkX does for comparison
- What happens if the value is too large or too small

## Current Constants

```rust
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_ALPHA: f64 = 0.85;          // NX default is also 0.85
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 100; // NX default is also 100
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 1.0e-6;     // NX default is also 1e-6
const KATZ_DEFAULT_ALPHA: f64 = 0.1;                // NX default is also 0.1
const KATZ_DEFAULT_BETA: f64 = 1.0;                 // NX default is also 1.0
const KATZ_DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 1000;    // NX default is also 1000
const KATZ_DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 1.0e-6;         // NX default is also 1e-6
const HITS_DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 100;     // NX default is also 100
const HITS_DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 1.0e-8;         // NX default is also 1e-8
const DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-12;   // Our own threshold
const FLOW_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-10;                  // Our own threshold
const ASSORTATIVITY_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-15;         // Our own threshold
const PARAM_SUM_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-6;              // Same as NX
```

## Why This Matters

These constants define the numerical behavior of the entire algorithm library.
Without documentation, future contributors might:
- Change PAGERANK_DEFAULT_ALPHA without realizing it breaks NX parity
- Tighten FLOW_EPSILON to 1e-15 causing infinite loops in min_cost_flow
- Loosen DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON causing non-deterministic shortest paths

## Files To Change

- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (lines 15-27)

## Template

```rust
/// Damping factor for PageRank. NetworkX default: 0.85.
/// Higher values weight link structure more; lower values bias toward uniform.
/// Must match NX for parity — do not change without updating conformance fixtures.
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_ALPHA: f64 = 0.85;
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:57.639838945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:17.235104755Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:17.234589159Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive doc comments to all 13 constants in fnx-algorithms. Each documents: purpose, NX default match, what happens if changed, and why this specific value.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-ndm3","title":"NX-REPLACE: Graph products — cartesian, tensor, strong, lexicographic, corona, modular, rooted","description":"## Functions (7) — graph product implementations
1. cartesian_product — G□H: (g1,h1)~(g2,h2) iff g1=g2,h1~h2 OR g1~g2,h1=h2
2. tensor_product — G×H: (g1,h1)~(g2,h2) iff g1~g2 AND h1~h2
3. strong_product — G⊠H: union of cartesian and tensor products
4. lexicographic_product — G∘H: (g1,h1)~(g2,h2) iff g1~g2 OR (g1=g2 AND h1~h2)
5. corona_product — each node in G gets a complete copy of H
6. modular_product — (g1,h1)~(g2,h2) iff (g1~g2 AND h1~h2) OR (g1≁g2 AND h1≁h2)
7. rooted_product — each node in G gets a copy of H rooted at specified root

## Implementation plan
All products follow a common pattern:
1. Create nodes as (g, h) tuples for all g ∈ V(G), h ∈ V(H)
2. Add edges based on product-specific adjacency rules
3. Preserve node/edge attributes from source graphs

Rust implementation: create a generic product framework that takes an edge predicate, then instantiate for each product type. Nodes stored as 'g|h' strings in Rust, converted to Python tuples in the binding.

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_graph_products_parity.py:
- test_cartesian_product_paths: P2□P3 is a 2x3 grid
- test_cartesian_product_matches_nx: compare nodes/edges on 3+ graph pairs
- test_tensor_product_matches_nx: compare on K3×K3, P3×C4
- test_strong_product_is_union: verify G⊠H = G□H ∪ G×H
- test_strong_product_matches_nx: compare on small graphs
- test_lexicographic_product_matches_nx: compare on P3∘K2
- test_corona_product_matches_nx: compare node/edge counts
- test_modular_product_matches_nx: compare on small graphs
- test_rooted_product_matches_nx: compare on P3 rooted at 0
- test_node_keys_are_tuples: verify product nodes are Python tuples
- test_edge_attributes_preserved: verify source attrs carry through

Run: pytest tests/python/test_graph_products_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: add 7 product functions
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add 7 bindings with tuple node keys
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire 7 functions
- tests/python/test_graph_products_parity.py: new test file (11+ tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:40.268274007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:43.595286955Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:43.595105031Z","close_reason":"Replaced graph product NetworkX round-trips with direct FNX implementations and added parity coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-ndm3","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-okpa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:20.308135797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"franken_networkx-o7yo","title":"parity: Graph and DiGraph adj should expose mapping helpers","description":"Graph.adj and DiGraph.adj currently miss NetworkX's mapping-style helpers items(), keys(), and values().
